summary: Matched the Final Destination stage unit, src/melee/gr/grlast.c, converting it from NonMatching to Matching and replacing placeholder declarations with typed static stage logic, data tables, callbacks, map/last ground variables, animation/material transitions, particle-generator handling, camera/setup code, and supporting prototype/struct fixes. The decomp.dev report recorded 23 new grlast function matches and +8292 matched code bytes.
what_changed: configure.py changed Object(NonMatching, "melee/gr/grlast.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/gr/grlast.c"), marking the Final Destination stage as matched.

src/melee/gr/grlast.c expanded from mostly placeholders into a full 992-line implementation with stage setup, StageCallbacks, StageData, lifecycle callbacks, map state machine logic, animation selection, material/color transitions, camera calls, and particle-generator cleanup/updates.
grlast.h replaced broad UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS public-looking prototypes with typed static declarations such as Ground_GObj*, bool, DynamicsDesc*, Vec3*, HSD_JObj*, GXColor, and explicit argument lists.

types.h removed the old tiny grLast_GroundVars bitfield and added two more precise structs: Last_GroundVars for rotating/particle-linked stage objects and Map_GroundVars for Final Destination map state, child ground object slots, timers, flags, and color interpolation state.
types.h also changed UnkStage6B0 color-like fields from s32 to GXColor, with matching Ground_801C052C through Ground_801C06A4 prototypes and implementations updated from s32* to GXColor*.

grAnime prototypes were refined: grAnime_801C7FF8 and grAnime_801C8098 use int/float signatures, grAnime_801C8318 now returns HSD_AObj* instead of int, and missing declarations for grAnime_801C7980 and grAnime_801C7A94 were added.
gm_8016ECE8 was typed as float gm_8016ECE8(void), matching grlast's use of it as a floating difficulty/progress-like threshold input.
grLib_801C9854 and hsd_8039D5DC were typed to take HSD_JObj* instead of s32, matching the grlast call sites that pass jobj pointers.
psappsrt.h refined UnkGeneratorMember layout by replacing padding at x14 with Vec3 x14 and exposing x30 as a byte, supporting grlast writes to generator member position/rotation-style fields.

config/GALE01/symbols.txt adjusted Final Destination-related data/rodata/sdata/sdata2 symbol sizes and split some data regions into string labels and tables, aligning symbol metadata with the matched source.
smart_moves: The PR did not only write grlast.c; it corrected upstream helper prototypes and structs so the stage code could use real types instead of casts or placeholder signatures.

Separating Final Destination state into Last_GroundVars and Map_GroundVars made the union accesses in grlast explicit: u.last for rotating/object generator state and u.map for the map state machine and child ground objects.
The grlast callbacks were made static and typed in the local header, reflecting file-local stage callback usage and avoiding inaccurate exported UNK_RET declarations.

The implementation kept assert line numbers and diagnostics such as HSD_ASSERT(0x50), HSD_ASSERT(0x3F4), and OSReport("%s:%d: couldn t get gobj(id=%d)\n", __FILE__, 255, id), which are often important for matching and for preserving original behavior.

The data-table work paired source definitions such as StageCallbacks grLast_803E7EBC, StageData grNLa_803E7F90, Vec3 constants, S16Vec arrays, and animation-index tables with symbol-size corrections in symbols.txt.

Local constants expressed angle limits and rates using M_PI-derived float expressions, while runtime randomness used HSD_Randf/HSD_Randi, matching the observed Final Destination rotation behavior without hiding magic values behind invented names.

The code reused existing stage/ground helpers for common operations: Ground_801C14D0, Ground_801C2BA4, Ground_801C4A08, grAnime_801C7FF8, grAnime_801C8098, grMaterial_801C9604, grLib_801C96F8, and grLib_801C9834.
decomp_lessons: A stage match may require broad type cleanup outside the target file: ground color accessors, animation return types, game-mode scalar returns, particle structs, and grlib wrappers all had to become correctly typed for grlast to compile and match cleanly.
When a stage has multiple conceptual state blocks sharing Ground::u, split the union into stage-specific structs rather than forcing unrelated fields into one old placeholder struct.
GXColor fields should be represented as GXColor, not s32, when helper functions copy color structs through pointers and stage code interpolates r/g/b channels.
Particle/generator code benefits from exposing only the fields proven by use: this PR refined UnkGeneratorMember enough to support x8, x14, x30, and xA2 usage without overnaming the whole struct.
For stage data, matching source and symbol metadata should move together; data size and string/table boundary corrections in symbols.txt are part of the decomp, not cleanup afterthoughts.

Static callback declarations in stage headers can be useful for one-file stage implementations when the stage's callback table needs forward declarations but the functions are not intended as global API.
Avoid leaving helper APIs as integer placeholders once call sites prove pointer or object types; accurate prototypes reduce casts and can unlock matching in other units.
naming_conventions: Final Destination stage functions use the address-based grLast_8021xxxx naming pattern.
Unidentified Final Destination data symbols retain grNLa_803Exxxx / grNLa_804Dxxxx names when no semantic name is proven.
File-local helper functions use static declarations; one helper retains the fn_8021A9AC address-style name.
Ground union fields are grouped by stage-purpose names, specifically u.last for Final Destination object/generator motion data and u.map for map-wide state.
Bitfield names in Map_GroundVars preserve offset/bit evidence, e.g. xC4_b0, xC4_b1, xC4_b2_25, xC4_b26, and xC4_b27, instead of inventing semantic names.
Address comments are retained in headers, e.g. /* 21B920 */ static void grLast_8021B920(Ground_GObj*, int);
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted grlast.c only after all referenced callback, animation, ground, particle, and game-mode prototypes were precise enough to match the target code.
Used explicit StageCallbacks and StageData initializers to reproduce the original stage callback table and data layout.
Matched rodata/data/sdata/sdata2 boundaries by correcting symbol sizes and splitting string labels from adjacent tables in config/GALE01/symbols.txt.
Preserved original assert/report line constants and file-reporting strings, which can affect data and string layout.
Represented local angle constants with float expressions based on M_PI, including positive and negative bounds, to fit the generated floating-point constants used by the wobbling/rotation logic.
Introduced a small static inline grlib_inline wrapper around grLib_801C9834 and clearing gp->u.last.xE0, suggesting an inlining/control-flow tactic needed by the grLast_8021B920 state machine.
Changed grAnime_801C8318 to return HSD_AObj* so grlast could store the result, assert it, and manipulate animation object timing directly.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported 23 new matches in main/melee/gr/grlast, raising matched code by +8292 bytes and linked code by +8496 bytes for GALE01.
Largest newly matched functions reported by the bot were grLast_8021B920 (+2796 bytes), grLast_8021ADD0 (+1132 bytes), grLast_8021B5C4 (+860 bytes), and grLast_8021B2E8 (+732 bytes).
follow_up_queries: Find other stage PRs that split Ground::u into stage-specific structs like Last_GroundVars and Map_GroundVars.
Search for remaining s32* color accessors that should be GXColor* in ground or stage code.
Search for UnkGeneratorMember users to refine x14 and x30 semantics after this psappsrt.h change.
Compare other gr*.c stage matches that use StageCallbacks arrays plus StageData symbol-size corrections.
Check whether grAnime_801C8318 return type HSD_AObj* enabled matches in non-grlast units.
